---
title: "Lecture Notes"
---

::: {.lecture-list}
### Introduction {.unnumbered}
Lecture 01: Introduction: What is Physical AI?<br>
Lecture 02: Anatomy of a Physical AI System<br>

### Embodiment, Sensors, and Actuation {.unnumbered}
Lecture 03: Robot Bodies and Embodiment<br>
Lecture 04: Sensors: How Robots Perceive the World<br>
Lecture 05: Actuators: How Robots Move<br>

### Modeling, Perception, and Planning {.unnumbered}
Lecture 06: Basic Kinematics and Dynamics<br>
Lecture 07: Perception and World Understanding<br>
Lecture 08: From Goals to Actions: Planning and Policy<br>

### Control, Learning, and Physics {.unnumbered}
Lecture 09: Feedback Control<br>
Lecture 10: Learning for Physical AI<br>
Lecture 11: Physics for Physical AI<br>
Lecture 12: Integrating Data and Physics<br>

### Applications and Future {.unnumbered}
Lecture 13: Applications and Societal Impact<br>
Lecture 14: Final Presentations / Future of Physical AI<br>
:::
